PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-23-2026 Continued To Jun-01-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ryan Jorgenson appeared via zoom for James Logan (petitioner, objector).
Amanda Ebey appeared via zoom for Patrick Makuakane(petitioner, objector).

Ms. Ebey stated that her client had surgery, that they couldn't conduct mediation yet, and that she is requesting more time.

The Court shall grant the Joint Stipulation Order to Amend Mediation Pursuant to Breslin. Mediation is to be completed by May 22nd 2026 a compliance hearing on the outcome of mediation is set for June 1, 2026 at 9:00am in department 204.

All matters are continued to June 1, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Wu. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Continued To Mar-23-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Amanda Ebey appeared via zoom for and with Patrick Makuakane (petitioner).
David Simon appeared via zoom for and with James Logan (petitioner).

Ms. Ebey stated that the lost will copy was a screen shot from a video that was conveyed electronically.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 4, 2026. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 12, 2026. The matter is continued to and the Parties are to return on March 23, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Continued To Mar-23-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Continued To Dec-03-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Amanda Ebey for petitioner and objector Patrick Makuakane (via Zoom).
David Simon for petitioner and objector James Logan (via Zoom).

Ms. Ebey reported the discovery of a video recorded by the decedent in which he displayed, discussed, and signed a handwritten holographic will. She stated the will appears to revoke prior testamentary instruments, disinherits James Logan, and leaves the estate primarily to the decedent's nieces. A screenshot of the holographic will, taken from the video, was e-filed with a typed transcription, but the original document has not been located. Ms. Ebey stated she intends to file an amended petition to probate the later-dated holographic will and requested that the matter be continued.

Mr. Simon did not object to the requested continuance.

The Court continued the matter to December 3, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
